prompts: The unit circle is a circle with a radius of 1 centered at the origin of a coordinate plane. It is used to define the trigonometric functions sine, cosine, and tangent based on the coordinates and slope of angles measured from the positive x-axis. Angles in standard position, usually measured in radians, are marked around the circumference. Sine corresponds to the y-coordinate, cosine to the x-coordinate, and tangent to the slope (y/x) of a terminal ray. Key angles include 0, π/6, π/4, π/3, π/2, and their corresponding points on the circle.
